title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Program Brief: Input Replay
summary: Rendering consumes random draws, replay stores key state without timing, floats are compared as formatted text, settings overwrite atomically unsafe, and corrupted saves silently reset. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the input replay increment in reproducible replay and versioned local state. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Input Replay · Theory Input Replay
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3131 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 3131 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone. A defensible solution applies Input Replay, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reproducible replay and versioned local state.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-determinism-replay-save-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-determinism-replay-save-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_determinism_replay_save_4(seed: int = 4217, input_frames: int = 3600, replay_frames: int = 3600, state_hash_equal: bool = True, schema_version: int = 2, corrupted_save_rejected: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if seed < 0 or input_frames < 1:
        return False, input_frames, "replay-domain"
    if replay_frames != input_frames:
        return False, replay_frames, "replay-length"
    if not state_hash_equal:
        return False, replay_frames, "simulation-drift"
    if schema_version < 1 or not corrupted_save_rejected:
        return False, schema_version, "save-contract"
    assert replay_frames == input_frames
    return True, schema_version, "reproducible-run"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Input Replay model · Theory Input Replay
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Input Replay: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4700 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Competency probe: Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
